findings.title: Hardcoded credentials
findings.description: The secret key for JWT token generation is hardcoded.
findings.suggested_fix: Use environment variables or a secure secret management system to store sensitive credentials.

findings.title: Insecure password storage
findings.description: Passwords are stored in plain text in the in-memory database.
findings.suggested_fix: Use a secure password hashing algorithm like bcrypt or Argon2 to store passwords.

findings.title: Missing input validation
findings.description: The login endpoint does not validate user input for potential security vulnerabilities.
findings.suggested_fix: Implement input validation using a library like Pydantic or Cerberus to ensure user input conforms to expected formats.

findings.title: Insecure JWT token generation
findings.description: The JWT token is generated using a hardcoded secret key and the HS256 algorithm, which is vulnerable to certain attacks.
findings.suggested_fix: Use a secure secret key and consider using a more secure algorithm like RS256 or ES256.

findings.title: Missing authentication
findings.description: The login endpoint does not authenticate users before generating a JWT token.
findings.suggested_fix: Implement authentication using a library like FastAPI-Security or Authlib to ensure only authorized users can generate JWT tokens.

findings.title: Insecure error handling
findings.description: The login endpoint raises an HTTPException with a generic error message, which could potentially reveal sensitive information.
findings.suggested_fix: Implement custom error handling using a library like FastAPI-Error or Errorhandler to provide more informative and secure error messages.

findings.title: In-memory database
findings.description: The application uses an in-memory database, which is not suitable for production environments.
findings.suggested_fix: Consider using a production-ready database like PostgreSQL or MySQL to store user data.
